Point-based clothing deformation is learned with the summation of loss functions, including data and regularization terms.
The data term, Ldata, is a part of the overall loss term, L, which is calculated as L = Ldata + λrglLrgl. The weight λrgl is used to balance the loss terms.
The regularization term, Lrgl, is another part of the overall loss term, L, where L = Ldata + λrglLrgl. The weight λrgl is used to balance the loss terms.

The data term, Ldata, is further broken down into λpLp and λnLn, with Lp representing the normalized Chamfer distance for minimizing bi-directional distances between prediction point sets and ground-truth scans.
The data term, Ldata, also includes the normal loss term, Ln, which calculates the averaged L1 distance between predicted normals and their nearest ground-truth counterparts.
The normalized Chamfer distance, Lp, is used to minimize the bi-directional distances between point sets of predictions and ground-truth scans, leading to more accurate predictions.
The normal loss term, Ln, measures the L1 distance between predicted normals and their nearest ground-truth counterparts, ensuring the accuracy of the predicted normals.
Regularization terms are added to prevent the predicted deformations from becoming extremely large, ensuring more stable and realistic results.
Regularization terms also aim to regularize the garment code, ensuring that the code remains within acceptable and manageable ranges.
The regularization on the pose-dependent displacement encourages it to be as small as possible, implying that pose-invariant deformation should be retained in the template displacement
The regularization term is aimed at retaining pose-invariant deformation in the garments, represented by the clothing deformation in various poses
The regularization term implies that the pose-invariant deformation should be retained in the template displacement, forming the garment-related template shared by all poses
